sglang.title: [router] support http2 in router
sglang.author: zhuzilin
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Make the router support http2 and http1.x at the same time, so that the concurrency of the router on single node won't be limited by the available ports.
Thank you for your time on reviewing this pr :)

sglang.title: [RL] allow weight updation with dp attention enabled
sglang.author: zhuzilin
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Allow running RL training with --enable-dp-attention.
Modifications

The origin limit for self.server_args.dp_size == 1 is to make sure that where are only tp_size workers/gpus within the server. With dp attention enabled, this requirement is still met.

QoQ of QServe is a great W4A8 quantization method. We integrate it into SGLang now.  This PR as a part of #6349 mainly integrates qserve w4a8 gemm into sgl-kernel.
Thanks @mit-han-lab for their great contribution. You can refer to their paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.04532 for more deta

sglang.title: [PD] Abort request if transfer fails
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

The current transfer failure handling is a placeholder. The correct way to do it is

Raise the real exception from sender/receiver in failure_exception()
When a failure status is polled, abort the request immediately and log the error

In additional,

vllm.author: kebe7jun

See: discussion, The --device arg is actually useless and potentially misleading, so let's remove it or mark it as deprecated?
cc @DarkLight1337
FIX #18301

vllm.title: [V1] Fix general plugins not loaded in engine for multiproc
vllm.author: shinoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vLLM general plugins are primarily used to register custom, out-of-tree (OOT) models.
Currently, when multi-processing is used (e.g. TP>1), plugins will not be loaded at the engine level. This is because general plugins are only loaded at the worker level here. This is ok when using UniProcExecutor

vllm.title: [ROCm][Kernel][V1] Enable AMD Radeon GPU Custom Paged Attention on v1
vllm.author: hyoon1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add additional custom paged attention kernels for AMD gfx11/gfx12 GPU support. Based on PRs:  #12348 #15720 #13843
Due to the differences in architecture from MI, specific instructions and detailed logic have changed (mfma16 -> wmma16/wmma16_gfx12), so new kernels for each architecture has been adde

vllm.title: [FEAT][ROCm] Upgrade AITER MLA v1 backend
vllm.author: vllmellm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR upgrades the AITER MLA attention backend on the v1 engine, as this backend was previously upgraded on the v0 engine in this PR. The upgrade uses AITER commit c1debd8, which introduces the relevant API changes.
It's important to note that the mla_decode_fwd kernel in the AITER package imposes

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Consistent ascii handling in tool parsers
vllm.author: schoennenbeck
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR makes the json-dumping of arguments in the llama3_json tool call parser consistent with that of the hermes and mistral parsers in that it uses ensure_ascii=False within json.dumps. This is necessary since the OpenAIServingChat compares already yielded strings to new strings using json.dumps(

vllm.title: [FalconH1] Fix output dtype in RMSNorm fallback path for Falcon-H1 (e.g. 0.5B)
vllm.author: dhiaEddineRhaiem
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR:

Fixes a dtype mismatch error originating from out_proj(hidden_states) where hidden_states (output of the RMS gating path) remained in fp32 when use_rms_norm is set to False (e.g. tiiuae/Falcon-H1-0.5B-Base) , causing incompatibility with bfloat16 weights. The fix ensures hidden_states is c

vllm.title: [CI] Fix race condition with StatelessProcessGroup.barrier
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
First, this includes one small security fix to the test_shm_broadcast
test.  Don't use the host's default IP address for this test. Use
localhost.  Otherwise we are binding a socket to an IP address that is
externally reachable. If the host does not have a proper firewall
configuration and is expose

vllm.title: Intialize io_thread_pool attribute in the beginning.
vllm.author: rabi
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We check for io_thread_pool attribute in shutdown()  before it could be initialized and results in AttributeError. Should be intialized to None in the beginning before we create a ThreadPoolExecutor.
FIX #18302

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Inconsistent token calculation compared to HF in llava family
vllm.author: cyr0930
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Logic in vllm flooring the value and logic in transformers rounding the value.
Following hf-transformers logic would be better as follows
(https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/llava_onevision/processing_llava_onevision.py#L269)

vllm.title: [BugFix][DP] Send DP wave completion only from `dp_rank==0`
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We were sending this notification back to the front-end for local_dp_rank==0 rather than dp_rank==0 which in multi-node case could result in redundant messages.
Since the wave count is included in the message explicitly, this doesn't actually cause any functional issues.
Thanks to @kouroshHakha for

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Model] Make Olmo2Model weight loading return loaded weights
vllm.author: 2015aroras
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Issue: OLMo 2 models recently started to fail to load. The root cause is that OLMo 2 does not track initialized weights, but #18113 caused Olmo2ForCausalLM to try return the set of initialized weights. Since  Olmo2Model, which has the bulk of the weight loading logic, does not return the set of init
